summary: This page discusses signal power for complex signals, defining it as the long-term time average of the squared magnitude and relating it to the rms value. It explains when such averages exist, contrasting finite-energy and finite-power signals, and notes that some signals such as a ramp have neither finite energy nor finite power. The page also comments that signal energy and power are mathematical measures of signal size or capability rather than actual physical energy, and highlights their use in error analysis and communication systems.

figures.caption: Examples of Signals: (a) a signal with finite energy (b) a signal with finite power.
